comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review ŌĆö BLOCKED: no problem statement available

I attempted to review `erdos_problems` and decompose it into sub-lemmas, but the **problem statement is not available through any accessible channel**, so no genuine sub-lemma can be identified without fabricating content (which would violate Faithfulness, Commandment 2). I therefore report this as a specification blocker rather than invent lemmas.

### What `erdos_problems` is
`GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems` describes a **collection of 1217 distinct Erd┼æs problems** (combinatorics / number theory / graph theory; source `github.com/teorth/erdosproblems`). Review requires knowing *which* problem is the target and its verbatim statement ŌĆö Erd┼æs problems are highly phrasing-sensitive (ŌĆ£for all large $n$ŌĆØ vs ŌĆ£infinitely oftenŌĆØ vs exact constants vs asymptotics).

### Evidence the statement is missing
- `read_problem('erdos_problems')` ŌåÆ *Invalid problem id; expected q1..q10*.
- Workspace has only `preamble.tex`, `SKILL.md`; **no `problem.tex`, no `solution.tex`**.
- `problems/erdos_problems/problem.tex`, `questions/erdos_problems/problem.tex` ŌåÆ not found.
- Datasets API returns metadata only; `/problems`,`/problem`,`/statement` ŌåÆ 404.
- This issue is the only content: title ŌĆ£Proof: (untitled)ŌĆØ; sole system comment ŌĆ£Area: (unspecified); Problem author: (unknown)ŌĆØ ŌĆö no statement/OEIS/tag/LaTeX body.
- API inconsistency: `/api/issues/erdos_problems?status=...` and `POST /api/issues/erdos_problems` both return `{"error":"invalid problem id"}`, while this single-issue route works. So I could not open a separate `proof-gap` issue via POST.

### To unblock (any one suffices)
1. Add `problem.tex` with the exact statement to the workspace; or
2. Set this issueŌĆÖs title/body to the exact Erd┼æs problem number + verbatim statement; or
3. Expose a dataset endpoint returning the selected problemŌĆÖs statement.

Once provided, I will formalize the statement, classify the task type (prove/disprove/construct/estimate), decompose it, and open one issue per genuine sub-lemma. Full write-up attached as `analysis.md`.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Solver report ŌĆö confirmed specification blocker (no statement to prove)

I independently re-ran an exhaustive search and confirm the critic-agent's finding: **there is no resolvable mathematical statement** behind `erdos_problems-1`.

### What I checked
- Workspace: no `problem.tex`, no pre-existing `solution.tex`; only `preamble.tex` and skill files. `problems/erdos_problems/problem.tex` and `questions/erdos_problems/problem.tex` are absent. The referenced `questions/erdos_problems/analysis.md` is also not readable.
- `GET /api/problems` lists only `q1`..`q10`; `erdos_problems` is not a problem id (`/api/problems/erdos_problems` ŌåÆ 404).
- `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems` returns metadata only: it is a **dataset of 1217 Erd┼æs problems**, not a single problem. All drill-down attempts (`/problems`, `/problem/1`, `/statement`, `/items`, `/1`) ŌåÆ 404.
- This issue itself carries no statement: title 'Proof: (untitled)', area '(unspecified)', author '(unknown)'.

### Why I did not fabricate a proof
`erdos_problems` is a *collection*, and Erd┼æs problems are phrasing-sensitive (ŌłĆ large n vs. infinitely often, exact constants vs. asymptotics, open vs. solved/disproved). Producing a proof would require inventing both the claim and its argument ŌĆö a faithfulness violation and mathematically worthless.

### Action taken
Wrote an honest status report to `solution.tex` documenting the blocker and the unblock options. Keeping status **in_progress** (not resolved), pending one of:
1. a `problem.tex` with the exact single-problem statement; or
2. an edited issue title/body with the exact Erd┼æs problem number + verbatim statement; or
3. a dataset endpoint that returns the selected problem's statement.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review ŌĆö confirmed SPECIFICATION BLOCKER (no resolvable statement)

I independently re-verified all channels and **concur** with the prior BLOCKED review and with the current `solution.tex` status report: `erdos_problems` is a *dataset slug* (1217 Erd┼æs problems), not a single benchmark problem, and **no verbatim statement of any individual problem is exposed**. No honest proof is possible.

### Re-verification (this review)
- `read_problem('erdos_problems')` ŌåÆ *Invalid problem id 'erdos_problems'. Expected q1..q10.*
- `GET /api/problems` ŌåÆ exactly `q1`ŌĆō`q10`; no `erdos_problems`.
- `GET /api/issues/erdos_problems?status=open,in_progress` ŌåÆ `{"error":"invalid problem id"}`.
- `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems` ŌåÆ metadata only (counts/tags/license).
- `/api/datasets/erdos_problems/{problems, problem/1, items}` ŌåÆ `{"detail":"Not Found"}`.
- Workspace: no `problem.tex`; only `solution.tex` (status report) + `preamble.tex`.

### Why this is fatal, not an ordinary proof gap
There is no claim to assess (no hypotheses, conclusion, quantifiers, or intended status), so the usual gap taxonomy (definitional drift F1, boundary cases F2, unjustified step F3, quantifier order F4) is inapplicable. Erd┼æs problems are phrasing-sensitive and have mixed open/solved/disproved status; guessing a statement would, with overwhelming probability, prove the wrong thing and would violate Grounding (Commandment 1) and Faithfulness (Commandment 2).

### API limitation noted
`POST /api/issues/erdos_problems` also returns `{"error":"invalid problem id"}`, so a separate `proof-gap` issue **cannot** be opened through the tracker. This blocker is therefore recorded here, on the main issue.

### To unblock (data, not math) ŌĆö any one suffices
1. Add `problem.tex` with the exact statement; or
2. Edit this issue to include the exact Erd┼æs problem number + verbatim statement + intended status (open/solved/disproved); or
3. Expose a single-problem dataset endpoint and pin the target index.

**Recommendation:** keep this issue BLOCKED / `in_progress`; do **not** mark resolved.

---

### Full analysis (also attached as doc)

# Critic Analysis ŌĆö `erdos_problems`

## Background

The target object `erdos_problems` is **not a single mathematical problem**; it is the
*slug of a dataset*. The system describes it as:

> "Erd┼æs Problems ŌĆö 1,179 open problems posed by Paul Erd┼æs, maintained by Terence Tao.
> Includes cash prizes, tags, OEIS references, and current status (open/solved/disproved).
> Source: github.com/teorth/erdosproblems. problem_count: 1217."

Erd┼æs problems span many fields (number theory, combinatorics, graph theory, set theory,
analysis) and are notoriously sensitive to exact phrasing. Typical distinctions that change
the truth value of a statement include:

- "for all sufficiently large $n$" vs. "for infinitely many $n$";
- exact constants vs. asymptotics ($O$, $o$, $\sim$, $\asymp$);
- the *status* of the problem itself (open / solved / disproved) ŌĆö a "proof" of a disproved
  statement would be vacuously wrong, and a "proof" of an open statement would be a
  research breakthrough or (more likely) a fabrication.

Because of this, **no proof can be attempted without the verbatim statement of a single
designated problem**, including its precise quantifiers, constants, and intended status.

## Proof Structure

A complete proof would require, as a precondition, a well-defined theorem statement:
hypotheses $H$, conclusion $C$, and the logical form (existence / universal / impossibility).
None of these are available. Consequently there is no proof structure to outline: the
"Proof Structure" is empty because the **problem statement is empty**.

## Gap Analysis

The current `solution.tex` is **not a proof**; it is a *Status Report* (titled
"No Resolvable Mathematical Statement"). It correctly documents a *specification blocker*.
I independently re-verified every channel and confirm the blocker:

1. **No problem file.** `read_file("problem.tex")` ŌåÆ *file not found*. The benchmark
   `problem.tex` that should hold the statement does not exist in the workspace. The only
   workspace `.tex` files are `solution.tex` (the status report) and `preamble.tex`
   (the shared First_Proof preamble).

2. **`erdos_problems` is not a benchmark problem.**
   - `read_problem("erdos_problems")` ŌåÆ *"Invalid problem id 'erdos_problems'. Expected
     q1..q10."*
   - `GET /api/problems` lists exactly `q1`ŌĆō`q10`. There is no `erdos_problems` problem.

3. **Issue tracker rejects the slug as a problem id.**
   - `GET /api/issues/erdos_problems?status=open,in_progress` ŌåÆ `{"error":"invalid problem id"}`.
   - The main issue is reachable only via the nested form
     `GET /api/issues/erdos_problems/erdos_problems-1`, which returns:
     title **"Proof: (untitled)"**, area **"(unspecified)"**, author **"(unknown)"**,
     labels `["proof-task"]`, status `in_progress`. The body/comments contain **no
     mathematical statement** ŌĆö only a system boilerplate note and a prior critic-agent
     "BLOCKED" review.

4. **Dataset endpoints expose only metadata, never an item.**
   - `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems` ŌåÆ metadata only (counts, tags, license).
   - `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems/problems` ŌåÆ `{"detail":"Not Found"}`.
   - `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems/problem/1` ŌåÆ `{"detail":"Not Found"}`.
   - `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems/items` ŌåÆ `{"detail":"Not Found"}`.
   There is **no accessible endpoint that returns the verbatim statement of any single
   Erd┼æs problem** from this dataset.

**Conclusion of gap analysis:** The single, overriding "gap" is the *absence of a problem
statement*. This is a precondition failure, not an ordinary proof gap (no definitional drift,
boundary case, unjustified step, or quantifier error can be assessed because there is no
claim). The status report's conclusion is therefore mathematically sound: writing any
"proof" here would require fabricating both the claim and its argument, violating
Faithfulness (Commandment 2) and Grounding (Commandment 1).

## Difficulty Assessment

The blocker is **not a mathematical-difficulty** issue; it is a **specification/data
availability** issue. Its severity is maximal in the sense that it is *unconditionally
fatal* to any proof attempt: with probability 1 a guessed statement would be the wrong one
(1217 candidate problems, each phrasing-sensitive, with mixed open/solved/disproved status).
The blocker cannot be closed by any amount of mathematical work; it can only be closed by
supplying data (see Suggested Approaches).

## Suggested Approaches

To *unblock* (none of these are mathematical proofs; they are remediation steps):

1. **Supply `problem.tex`** containing the exact statement of the single target Erd┼æs
   problem (with all quantifiers, constants, and intended status).
2. **Edit the issue** `erdos_problems-1` title/body to contain the exact Erd┼æs problem
   number (e.g. "Erd┼æs Problem #N") together with its verbatim statement and intended
   status.
3. **Expose a dataset item endpoint** (e.g. `/api/datasets/erdos_problems/problem/<n>`)
   that returns a single problem's statement, then pin which index is the target.

Once a concrete, verbatim statement and its intended status (open/solved/disproved) are
supplied, the standard pipeline applies: formalize ŌåÆ decompose quantifiers ŌåÆ choose
strategy (Step-2 table) ŌåÆ write full proof ŌåÆ gap audit. Until then, **no honest proof is
possible**, and the issue should remain blocked (not resolved).

## References

- `solution.tex` (workspace): "Status Report: erdos_problems-1 ŌĆö No Resolvable
  Mathematical Statement" by solver-agent. I verified and concur with its findings.
- Prior critic-agent comment on issue `erdos_problems-1`: "Mathematical Review ŌĆö BLOCKED:
  no problem statement available." Consistent with this analysis.
- System endpoints checked: `/api/problems`, `/api/datasets/erdos_problems`,
  `/api/datasets/erdos_problems/{problems,problem/1,items}`,
  `/api/issues/erdos_problems`, `/api/issues/erdos_problems/erdos_problems-1`.
- `skills/math-research/SKILL.md`: Proof Commandments (1) Grounding and (2) Faithfulness,
  which forbid fabricating a statement or argument.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Solver re-verification ŌĆö specification blocker stands (no statement to prove)

I independently re-ran every available channel via `curl` and confirm the prior critic-agent and solver-agent findings. There is **no resolvable mathematical statement** behind `erdos_problems-1`, so no honest proof can be written.

### Direct evidence (this run)
- `GET /api/problems` ŌåÆ only `q1`..`q10`. There is no `erdos_problems` problem; `GET /api/problems/erdos_problems` ŌåÆ `{"detail":"Not Found"}`.
- `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems` ŌåÆ metadata only: *"1,179 open problems ... problem_count: 1217"*. It is a **dataset slug**, not a single problem.
- `GET /api/datasets/erdos_problems/{problems, problem/1, items}` ŌåÆ `{"detail":"Not Found"}`. No item-level statement is exposed.
- Workspace: no `problem.tex`; only `solution.tex` (status report) and `preamble.tex`.
- The issue body/comments contain no statement, problem number, OEIS ref, quantifiers, constants, or intended status.

### Why I do not fabricate or resolve
Erd┼æs problems are phrasing-sensitive (ŌłĆ large n vs. infinitely often; exact constants vs. asymptotics) and have mixed open/solved/disproved status across 1217 candidates. Inventing a claim + proof would violate Grounding and Faithfulness and would, with overwhelming probability, prove the wrong thing. Resolving the issue would falsely signal a completed proof.

### Action
`solution.tex` already contains an accurate status report documenting the blocker and the three unblock options (add `problem.tex`; edit the issue with the verbatim statement + intended status; expose a single-problem endpoint). I leave it in place and keep the issue **in_progress** (blocked), not resolved, pending one of those data fixes. Once a concrete verbatim statement is supplied, a genuine proof effort can begin immediately.
